title: Start Spreading The News

jokes.text: Don't tell me you're having lunch with Bev - After all this. - Imagine? No, I amputated those people years ago.
jokes.setup: Johnny asks if Moira is having lunch with Bev after their past issues
jokes.punchline: Moira uses 'amputated' instead of 'cut off' to describe ending relationships
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira malapropism that's perfectly in character - using medical terminology for social situations. Well-constructed and quotable.

jokes.text: But bev and her big bazoo will get the word out quicker than any group memorandum.
jokes.setup: Moira explaining why she called Bev
jokes.punchline: Uses 'big bazoo' (mouth) in pompous language to describe gossip spreading
jokes.explanation: Another Moira vocabulary moment - mixing slang with formal language in her characteristic way.

jokes.text: I don't see any breakfast. - Mimosas! We're celebrating!
jokes.setup: David points out there's no breakfast visible
jokes.punchline: Johnny reveals breakfast is just alcohol
jokes.explanation: Simple setup/punchline about day drinking as celebration. Functional but not particularly original.

jokes.text: David? You don't have to look quite so morose. You're with your family.
jokes.setup: Family trying to take a celebratory photo
jokes.punchline: David's visible reluctance/misery during what should be a happy moment
jokes.explanation: David's characteristic attitude during family moments. Good character consistency but predictable.

jokes.text: Ah, now that's a framer. You just deleted it.
jokes.setup: Johnny declares the photo is perfect ('a framer')
jokes.punchline: Moira immediately deletes it
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and economy - the immediate contradiction between Johnny's pride and Moira's accidental deletion.

jokes.text: Do I detect a scintilla of lavender? Isn't it funny how the air suddenly smells sweeter now that you know we're leaving.
jokes.setup: Moira waxing poetic about the air quality
jokes.punchline: Johnny's dry observation that everything seems better now that they're leaving
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Moira's flowery language and Johnny's practical cynicism about their departure.

jokes.text: Well, Johnny, if you wanna be an executive, you gotta drive like an executive. It might be a bit premature spending all this money.
jokes.setup: Roland explaining his expensive new truck purchase
jokes.punchline: Johnny's understated concern about premature spending
jokes.explanation: Roland's characteristic impulsiveness contrasted with Johnny's financial prudence. Good character work.

jokes.text: I went to the spa. Had a pedicure and a blow-out, at the same time.
jokes.setup: Jocelyn describing her spa experience
jokes.punchline: The absurd image of getting pedicure and hair done simultaneously
jokes.explanation: Funny visual image that fits Jocelyn's character - practical but awkward approach to luxury.

jokes.text: I told the salesman I was just taking it around the block. But then I thought, maybe some cinnamon buns might just buy us a sun roof.
jokes.setup: Roland's supposed to return the truck he's test driving
jokes.punchline: His plan to bribe the salesman with baked goods for car upgrades
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Roland's naive small-town logic and bargaining style. The escalation from test drive to bribery with baked goods is absurd but character-specific.

jokes.text: Your... Hair. Is in a ponytail because I didn't wash it.
jokes.setup: David notices something different about Stevie's appearance
jokes.punchline: Stevie's blunt admission it's just unwashed hair
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie deadpan delivery deflating David's attempt at observation. Excellent economy and character voice.

jokes.text: Worry is but undernourished enthusiasm.
jokes.setup: Johnny telling Moira not to worry
jokes.punchline: Moira's pretentious pseudo-philosophy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Moira quote - sounds profound but is essentially meaningless. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: What says, 'Patrick, you're gonna love New York' more: A gift card to Joe's pizza or tickets to 'wicked'?
jokes.setup: David asking for gift advice
jokes.punchline: The stereotypical NYC experiences he's considering
jokes.explanation: David's attempt to be thoughtful through stereotypical tourist experiences. Mild humor from the cliched options.

jokes.text: You're taking Patrick on a trip to New York? Uh, no. We're moving there with all of you! What? Do you think we're gonna stay here all by ourselves?
jokes.setup: Stevie assumes it's just a trip
jokes.punchline: David's indignant assumption everyone would move together
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection and David's characteristic assumption that everyone revolves around his plans.

jokes.text: I mean, I realized I didn't need to live in a big city. I... guess I just needed to know that I could.
jokes.setup: Stevie explaining her decision to stay
jokes.punchline: The philosophical distinction between needing to do something vs. needing to know you could
jokes.explanation: Thoughtful character moment with gentle humor in the self-awareness. Very quotable insight about personal growth.

jokes.text: Can we stop talking about this? I feel like I'm doing something wrong.
jokes.setup: Stevie accidentally reveals Patrick's house hunting
jokes.punchline: Her panic at realizing she's spilled a secret
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Stevie's discomfort with accidental gossip spreading.

jokes.text: So you're finally getting out. Yeah. Um... I should probably go talk to my husband.
jokes.setup: Stevie's casual comment about David leaving
jokes.punchline: David's awkward realization he needs to have a difficult conversation
jokes.explanation: Good awkward beat showing David's discomfort with relationship communication. The pause carries the humor.

jokes.text: Oh, Alexis, garbage day is the same for the whole street. You didn't need to bring your garbage all the way here.
jokes.setup: Twyla assumes Alexis brought actual garbage
jokes.punchline: It's actually designer clothes Alexis is giving away
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that plays on the value difference between what Twyla sees as garbage vs. Alexis's expensive clothes.

jokes.text: No, twy, I can't take your money. I've seen how people tip here.
jokes.setup: Twyla offering to pay for clothes
jokes.punchline: Alexis's awareness of poor tipping in small town
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about small town economics that shows Alexis's growing awareness and consideration.

jokes.text: Okay, well, if you don't do this for money I'm scared to know what you do do for money.
jokes.setup: Twyla says she doesn't waitress for money
jokes.punchline: Alexis implies something sinister about Twyla's real income source
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the lottery revelation. Alexis's worldliness making her assume the worst about innocent Twyla.

jokes.text: I won some money in the lottery a few years ago. Oh my god, Twy! Go you! Why did you not tell me this before?
jokes.setup: Twyla casually mentions lottery winnings
jokes.punchline: Alexis's excited reaction to what seems like modest good fortune
jokes.explanation: Sets up the bigger revelation. Alexis's enthusiasm for what she thinks is a small win.

jokes.text: I just won $92 million? You won $92 million? Oh, no. Sorry. Okay, because I literally was about to pass out. Yeah. No, no. I split the $92 million with another guy.
jokes.setup: Twyla mentions the massive lottery amount
jokes.punchline: The clarification that she split it, making it 'only' $46 million
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and misdirection. The casual way Twyla 'corrects' herself from $92M to $46M as if that's a meaningful distinction is brilliant. Perfect character moment showing her humility.

jokes.text: And if I've learned anything from how my mom spent the money I gave her, It's that money can buy a lot of snowmobiles, But it can't buy happiness.
jokes.setup: Twyla explaining her philosophy about money
jokes.punchline: The specific absurd image of buying 'a lot of snowmobiles' as wasteful spending
jokes.explanation: Perfect specificity makes this memorable. The image of excessive snowmobile purchases is both absurd and believable for someone who suddenly got rich.

jokes.text: This cute dress makes me smile. Hm. Not quite sure how that one got in there.
jokes.setup: Alexis going through clothes to give away
jokes.punchline: Finding something inappropriate/revealing mixed in with the regular clothes
jokes.explanation: Typical Alexis moment suggesting something scandalous in her wardrobe. Character-appropriate but not particularly original.

jokes.text: Oh, that photo of me sunbathing atop a giant VHS tape, was a staple of Times Square for a whole season.
jokes.setup: Moira remembering her Rose Video spokesperson days
jokes.punchline: The absurd mental image of her posing on a giant VHS tape
jokes.explanation: Great visual callback to the family's past wealth and Moira's dramatic tendencies. The specific image is both absurd and believable for 80s advertising.

jokes.text: A clangorous, vociferous yes.
jokes.setup: Moira accepting the Sunrise Bay offer
jokes.punchline: Her characteristically over-the-top vocabulary for a simple 'yes'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira vocabulary showcase. The redundancy of using two synonyms for 'loud' to say yes is quintessentially her character.

jokes.text: When forced to choose between Clifton and myself, they decided mine was the bigger name.
jokes.setup: Explaining why she got the role back
jokes.punchline: Moira's self-aggrandizing interpretation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Moira's need to frame everything as her triumph.

jokes.text: No, there are 30 diamonds on that thing.
jokes.setup: David assumes Moira is giving him the tennis bracelet
jokes.punchline: Moira's immediate clarification that it's too valuable to give away
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Moira's materialistic priorities. The specific diamond count adds believable detail.

jokes.text: 'Cause I know how flustered you can get When there's more than one thing on your plate. Hm. Unless it's food.
jokes.setup: Patrick explaining why he didn't mention the house earlier
jokes.punchline: David's literal interpretation about actual food plates
jokes.explanation: Great wordplay that's perfectly in character for David's food obsession and literal mind. Excellent economy and timing.

jokes.text: Um, no, uh, I love New York. It was just a weird time in my life.
jokes.setup: Patrick questioning David's feelings about New York
jokes.punchline: David's massive understatement about his past trauma there
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with David minimizing his past trauma. The understatement works because viewers know his history.

jokes.text: Um, no, uh, I love New York. It was just a weird time in my life.
jokes.setup: Patrick asking about David's NYC feelings
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement of his traumatic past
jokes.explanation: Typical David deflection - 'weird time' for losing fortune and lifestyle

jokes.text: Yeah, and by then you'll have met some people That would go and do that with you.
jokes.setup: David talking about going to Yankees games
jokes.punchline: His assumption that Patrick will need to find new friends since David won't go
jokes.explanation: David's self-awareness about his limitations as a partner combined with his assumption Patrick will easily replace him. Mildly cringey but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: I say, fuck 'em. / Veronica!
jokes.setup: Veronica cursing about Sunrise Bay
jokes.punchline: Jocelyn's scandalized reaction to the language
jokes.explanation: Solid character contrast between the Jazzagals members' personalities.

jokes.text: Well, aren't you a wolf pack by which any gal would be so lucky to be protected.
jokes.setup: The Jazzagals supporting Moira
jokes.punchline: Moira's over-dramatic metaphor comparing them to a wolf pack
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira grandiosity turning a simple thank you into an elaborate metaphor. Good character voice.

jokes.text: wolf pups are born blind and deaf, And in your care I have learned to see and hear the beauty in the bucolic.
jokes.setup: Moira extending the wolf metaphor
jokes.punchline: The elaborate biological comparison to her personal growth
jokes.explanation: Brilliant extension of the wolf metaphor with genuine emotional weight. Moira's vocabulary ('bucolic') and the biological accuracy make it both funny and touching.

jokes.text: Oh no. No. Let's not turn this into a hallmark movie of the week. I've done enough of those for a lifetime. Okay?
jokes.setup: The emotional moment between Moira and the Jazzagals
jokes.punchline: Moira's self-aware resistance to sentimentality, referencing her past TV movies
jokes.explanation: Perfect meta-joke that breaks the emotional tension while staying in character. The specific reference to Hallmark movies fits her career trajectory perfectly.

jokes.text: Oh. That I shouldn't be afraid to spend money On things that are special to me. So... I bought the café.
jokes.setup: Twyla saying Alexis's advice resonated about spending on special things
jokes.punchline: The massive escalation from 'spa day' advice to buying the entire café
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and misdirection. Twyla taking modest advice and applying it to a multi-million dollar purchase is perfectly absurd. Great callback structure.

jokes.text: Oh. That I shouldn't be afraid to spend money On things that are special to me. So... I bought the café.
jokes.setup: Twyla saying Alexis's advice resonated
jokes.punchline: Massive overreaction to 'spend a little money' advice
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - goes from spending 'a little bit' to buying an entire business

jokes.text: Twyla's café tropical. It's got a nice ring to it, right? Yeah. Or like 'Twyla's café'. Yeah, I don't think so.
jokes.setup: Twyla proposing an elaborate name for her café
jokes.punchline: Alexis suggesting the simpler option and Twyla immediately rejecting it
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Twyla's commitment to her elaborate vision despite Alexis's practical suggestion.

jokes.text: Even though I know I'm gonna regret it, In like a minute. I think that if I'm gonna do this on my own, I need to do this on my own.
jokes.setup: Alexis turning down Twyla's generous offer
jokes.punchline: Her immediate acknowledgment that she'll regret this decision
jokes.explanation: Great self-aware character moment. Alexis's growth is shown through her principle while her honesty about regretting it keeps it funny and real.

jokes.text: Hey Twy, like, cute dress. / Thanks. My friend gave it to me.
jokes.setup: David complimenting Twyla's dress
jokes.punchline: Twyla's response about her 'friend' (it's Alexis's dress)
jokes.explanation: Nice callback with sweet emotional undertone about their friendship.

jokes.text: How many years have I known you, And I still can't figure out what goes on inside your head. Join the club.
jokes.setup: Stevie expressing confusion about David's thought process
jokes.punchline: David's admission that he doesn't understand himself either
jokes.explanation: Perfect David self-awareness delivered with economy. The 'join the club' response is both funny and revealing about his character.

jokes.text: That was a joke, in case it didn't land.
jokes.setup: David making a joke that apparently fell flat
jokes.punchline: His awkward clarification that it was supposed to be funny
jokes.explanation: Good awkward David moment. The meta-commentary on joke failure is characteristic of his anxious personality.

jokes.text: Good people. Good people do things like that. / Hence the reason why we don't understand it.
jokes.setup: Discussing who does thoughtful gestures like Patrick
jokes.punchline: David's self-aware admission of their moral shortcomings
jokes.explanation: Excellent character development moment showing their growth and self-awareness.

jokes.text: Why do you wanna go back to a place That's done nothing but hurt your feelings? Because I want those people to know that I'm not a joke. And that I've won.
jokes.setup: Stevie questioning David's motivation to return to New York
jokes.punchline: David's vulnerable admission about wanting to prove himself to people who hurt him
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment that's both funny and deeply sad. David's need for validation from people who don't deserve it is painfully relatable.

jokes.text: Apparently there was an electronic music festival in Norway that took priority.
jokes.setup: David explaining why his NYC friends aren't coming to the wedding
jokes.punchline: The absurd priority of the music festival over his wedding
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing the shallowness of David's NYC connections.

jokes.text: Because I want those people to know that I'm not a joke. And that I've won.
jokes.setup: Alexis asking why he wants to return to a place that hurt him
jokes.punchline: David's honest admission about wanting to prove himself
jokes.explanation: Powerful character moment revealing David's deep insecurities and motivations.

jokes.text: Did you put on deodorant today? Excuse me? I know, it might be the farm. It's the farm. Okay. I smelled something. It's not me! All right! We're leaving! Relax.
jokes.setup: David detecting an unpleasant smell
jokes.punchline: The escalating argument about who or what smells, ending in their hasty retreat
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy beat that provides comic relief after the emotional conversation. The escalation and quick exit work well.

jokes.text: Wait. One pizza? What is this, les mis? Yes, it's a dignified family symposium, David. Not some all-one can- consume smorgasbord.
jokes.setup: David complaining about the small amount of food
jokes.punchline: His dramatic comparison to Les Misérables and Johnny's pompous response
jokes.explanation: Excellent David dramatics with the Les Mis reference, followed by Johnny's equally pretentious defense. Both characters perfectly in voice.

jokes.text: I don't think you can afford my quote anymore.
jokes.setup: Johnny suggesting they play charades
jokes.punchline: Moira treating family game night like a paid acting gig
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira moment treating everything like a professional engagement. The economy and character voice are excellent.

jokes.text: I don't think you can afford my quote anymore.
jokes.setup: Johnny suggesting they play charades
jokes.punchline: Moira treating family game night like a professional gig
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira treating everything like a business transaction, even family time.

jokes.text: I don't think you can afford my quote anymore. Just saying it out loud makes it so real.
jokes.setup: Family wants to play charades
jokes.punchline: Moira acts like professional actress too expensive for family game
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira delusion - treating family game like professional acting gig

jokes.text: You take that ember of independence and you keep it burning. Because you, my darling, are destined to be on fire!
jokes.setup: Alexis announcing she's going to New York alone
jokes.punchline: Moira's over-the-top motivational metaphor
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira inspirational speech with fire metaphor that goes perfectly over the top.

jokes.text: September 3rd. That's my wedding day. And we're tied to the third? - Oh Moira... - Am I tied to the third?
jokes.setup: Revealing the parents are leaving on David's wedding day
jokes.punchline: Moira's self-centered focus on her travel arrangements during the crisis
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Moira's priorities. The repetition of 'tied to the third' shows her focus on logistics over emotional impact.

jokes.text: It's just worth noting that the lie-flat seats were only on the evening flight.
jokes.setup: Family discussing leaving after the wedding
jokes.punchline: Moira's materialistic concern about flight comfort
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira priority - comfort amenities over family considerations.

jokes.text: David, I promise I will make you so happy here. You fuckin' better.
jokes.setup: Patrick's romantic promise about their future
jokes.punchline: David's characteristically blunt and unromantic response
jokes.explanation: Perfect David response to romantic sentiment - simultaneously sweet and completely unromantic. The contrast and economy make it memorable.

jokes.text: You know, at this point who even needs a wedding, Am I right? Oh, I do. I need a wedding. Big time.
jokes.setup: David suggesting they might not need a wedding
jokes.punchline: His immediate reversal showing how much he actually wants it
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing David's vulnerability beneath his cynicism. The quick reversal is both funny and touching.

tmdb_overview: Moira receives some unexpected news, and David makes a tough decision about where he wants to live.
insight: Series finale delivers emotional beats over laughs—lowest joke density of Season 6.
